stack.llm: Ollama on HOST (not Docker) - llama3.1:8b + llama3.2:3b

pipeline.stage_1_ingest: Sanitize, normalize, dedup, PII mask, skill retrieval, benign routing
pipeline.stage_2_analyze: Load saved plan from investigation_plans.json (Path A) OR LLM tool selection (Path C)
pipeline.stage_3_execute: In-process tool runner — chains 2-8 tools per plan with variable resolution
pipeline.stage_4_assess: Verdict derivation, IOC provenance validation, signal boost, risk floor, suppression detection
pipeline.stage_5_store: Persist results + audit events to PostgreSQL

critical_constraints: Tools are NEVER called individually — they are chained in investigation plans
The assess stage transforms risk_score and verdict — tool output alone is not the final result
Testing MUST go through the API (POST /api/v1/tasks), not by importing Python functions
investigation_plans.json keys are canonical plan names (e.g. phishing_investigation, not phishing)
analyze.py resolves task_type aliases to plan keys (e.g. phishing → phishing_investigation)
Benign routing uses inverted logic — alerts are benign by DEFAULT unless they match ATTACK_INDICATORS
Rate limit: 10 auth attempts per 15 minutes per IP — login once per test batch

key_files.HANDOVER.md: READ THIS FIRST — rules of engagement for AI assistants
key_files.worker/tools/investigation_plans.json: 24 attack plans — this is how tools map to attack types
key_files.worker/stages/assess.py: Verdict derivation, signal boost, risk floor
key_files.worker/stages/ingest.py: ATTACK_INDICATORS list, benign routing, content-based override
